title: Descaling Required
shortDescription: The machine requires descaling to remove mineral buildup.
fullDescription: The descale indicator on Philips fully automatic espresso machines triggers when the internal counter determines enough brews have been made to warrant descaling. Scale buildup in the thermoblock and internal water circuits reduces heating efficiency and flow rate.

causes.cause: Normal mineral buildup from regular use

fixSteps.title: Prepare descaling solution
fixSteps.instruction: Empty the water tank. Pour the Philips descaling solution into the tank and add water to the CALC CLEAN line.

fixSteps.title: Enter descaling mode
fixSteps.instruction: On the display, navigate to Maintenance > Start Calc Clean. Place a large container under the coffee spout and hot water outlet.

fixSteps.title: Run the cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Follow the on-screen prompts. The machine will alternate between pausing and dispensing solution through the brew group and steam circuit. Takes about 30 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Rinse thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: When prompted, empty the tank, rinse, fill with fresh water, and run the rinse cycle. The machine will indicate when complete.

whenToCallPro: Only if the indicator does not clear after completing the full cycle.
videoSearchQuery: Philips espresso machine descale Calc Clean how to

faq.q: How often should I descale?
faq.a: Every 5000 cups with AquaClean filter, or every 2-3 months without.

faq.q: Can I use third-party descaling solution?
faq.a: Philips recommends their own solution, but citric acid (25g per liter) works as an alternative.

faq.q: What is AquaClean?
faq.a: AquaClean is a water filter that reduces scale buildup, extending the time between descaling to 5000 cups.

title: Brew Group Not Detected or Needs Cleaning
shortDescription: The brew group is not properly installed or needs to be cleaned.
fullDescription: The brew group (brewing unit) icon indicates the removable brew group is not detected, improperly seated, or needs cleaning. The brew group is a mechanical assembly that compresses and brews the coffee.

causes.cause: Brew group not properly inserted after cleaning
Brew group dirty with old coffee oils and residue
Brew group rails or tracks obstructed

symptoms: Insert/remove brew group icon on display
Brew group does not slide in smoothly

fixSteps.title: Remove the brew group
fixSteps.instruction: Open the service door on the side of the machine. Press the red PUSH button and slide the brew group out.

fixSteps.title: Clean the brew group
fixSteps.instruction: Rinse the brew group under warm running water. Do NOT use soap or put it in the dishwasher. Clean the mesh filter and the funnel area.

fixSteps.title: Dry and lubricate
fixSteps.instruction: Let the brew group air dry. Apply a thin layer of food-safe silicone grease to the O-ring and rails (Philips provides grease with some models).

fixSteps.title: Reinsert the brew group
fixSteps.instruction: Slide the brew group back along the rails until it clicks into the locked position. Close the service door firmly.

whenToCallPro: If the brew group is physically damaged or the detection switch has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Philips espresso brew group cleaning removal how to

faq.q: How often should I clean the brew group?
faq.a: Weekly rinse under water, and monthly deep clean with lubrication.

faq.q: Can I put the brew group in the dishwasher?
faq.a: No. The brew group should only be rinsed under running water. Dishwasher detergent can damage the seals.

faq.q: Why does my brew group not slide in?
faq.a: The machine must be turned OFF before inserting the brew group. Also check for dried coffee residue on the rails.

title: Coffee Grinder Jammed or Blocked
shortDescription: The built-in ceramic grinder is jammed or not grinding properly.
fullDescription: The grinder error indicates the ceramic disc grinder cannot operate. This is typically caused by a foreign object in the beans, oily beans clogging the grinding mechanism, or the grind setting being too fine.

causes.cause: Foreign object (stone, twig) in the bean hopper

symptoms: Coffee is very coarse or inconsistent

fixSteps.title: Empty the bean hopper
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all beans from the hopper. Check for foreign objects like stones or twigs.

fixSteps.title: Adjust to a coarser setting
fixSteps.instruction: While the grinder is running (or the machine is on), turn the grind adjustment dial to a coarser setting.

fixSteps.title: Vacuum the grinder
fixSteps.instruction: Use a vacuum cleaner nozzle to suck out any stuck grounds from the hopper opening.

fixSteps.title: Clean with rice or grinder tabs
fixSteps.instruction: Put a tablespoon of uncooked white rice or Philips grinder cleaning tablets through the grinder to absorb oils and clear debris.

whenToCallPro: If the ceramic burrs are chipped or the grinder motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Philips espresso grinder jammed fix cleaning

faq.q: Do Philips grinders use ceramic or steel burrs?
faq.a: Philips uses ceramic disc grinders, which are quieter and more durable than steel.

faq.q: Should I adjust grind while running?
faq.a: Yes, Philips recommends adjusting the grind setting only while the grinder is running.

faq.q: Can oily beans damage the grinder?
faq.a: They do not damage the ceramic burrs, but they can clog the grinding mechanism. Clean regularly.

title: Grounds Container Full
shortDescription: The used coffee grounds container is full and needs emptying.
fullDescription: The grounds container indicator activates when the internal counter has tracked enough brew cycles since the last container emptying. The container must be emptied to prevent overflow.

causes.cause: Grounds container has accumulated used pucks
Grounds counter not reset after emptying

symptoms: Grounds overflow into the drip tray

fixSteps.title: Remove and empty the container
fixSteps.instruction: Open the service door and pull out the grounds container. Empty all used coffee grounds into a trash bin or compost.

fixSteps.title: Clean the container
fixSteps.instruction: Rinse the container with water. Wipe dry before reinserting.

fixSteps.title: Reinsert properly
fixSteps.instruction: Slide the grounds container back into position until it clicks. Close the service door fully.

fixSteps.title: Always empty when machine is ON
fixSteps.instruction: The counter resets when you remove and replace the container while the machine is on. Always empty while powered on.

videoSearchQuery: Philips espresso grounds container empty reset

faq.q: Why does the icon stay after emptying?
faq.a: The machine must be ON when you remove and reinsert the container for the counter to reset.

faq.q: How many brews before the container is full?
faq.a: Approximately 12-15 double espressos, depending on the model.

faq.q: Can used coffee grounds be composted?
faq.a: Yes, used coffee grounds are excellent for composting and gardening.

title: Water Tank Empty or Not Detected
shortDescription: The water tank needs refilling or is not properly seated on the machine.
fullDescription: The water tank indicator means the machine cannot detect water. The tank may be empty, improperly seated, or the water level sensor is obstructed.
symptoms: Machine will not brew or steam
Tank appears full but machine does not detect it

fixSteps.title: Refill and reseat the tank
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the water tank, fill to the MAX line with fresh water, and reinsert firmly until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Check the float valve
fixSteps.instruction: Look inside the tank for the float mechanism. Shake gently to ensure it moves freely.

fixSteps.title: Clean the tank connection area
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the bottom of the tank and the machine's water inlet with a clean cloth.

fixSteps.title: Remove the AquaClean filter temporarily
fixSteps.instruction: If installed, remove the AquaClean filter and test without it to rule out a filter issue.

whenToCallPro: If the water level sensor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Philips espresso fill water tank not detected fix

faq.q: Why does my Philips not detect the full water tank?
faq.a: The float valve may be stuck, or the tank is not seated properly. Remove, shake gently, and reinsert.

faq.q: Can I use filtered water?
faq.a: Yes, and it is recommended to reduce scale buildup. The AquaClean filter is designed for this.

faq.q: How often should I change the water?
faq.a: Change the water daily for best taste and hygiene.

title: LatteGo Milk System Not Detected or Blocked
shortDescription: The LatteGo milk frothing system is not properly connected or is clogged.
fullDescription: The LatteGo icon indicates the milk frothing system is not detected or cannot operate. The LatteGo consists of two snap-together parts that channel milk and air for automatic frothing. Residue buildup or improper assembly causes errors.

causes.cause: LatteGo parts not properly snapped together
Milk residue blocking the air inlet or milk channel
LatteGo not properly seated on the machine
LatteGo detection magnet or sensor issue

symptoms: No milk dispensed during milk drink recipes

fixSteps.title: Disassemble and clean LatteGo
fixSteps.instruction: Separate the two LatteGo parts by pulling them apart. Wash both parts under warm running water. Use the provided cleaning pin to clear the milk outlet.

fixSteps.title: Reassemble properly
fixSteps.instruction: Snap the two LatteGo parts firmly together. You should hear a click. Ensure the milk spout is aligned with the dispensing position.

fixSteps.title: Reseat on the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Place the LatteGo assembly on the machine in the correct position. It should snap into place magnetically.

fixSteps.title: Run a milk rinse
fixSteps.instruction: Run the rinse/clean cycle from the machine menu to flush any remaining residue from the milk circuit.

whenToCallPro: If the LatteGo detection sensor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Philips LatteGo not working fix clean milk system

faq.q: How often should I clean the LatteGo?
faq.a: After every use. Disassemble and rinse under running water immediately after making milk drinks.

faq.q: Can I put LatteGo in the dishwasher?
faq.a: Yes, the LatteGo parts are dishwasher safe.

faq.q: Why does my milk have no foam?
faq.a: The air inlet may be blocked with dried milk. Clean thoroughly, paying attention to the small air holes.

title: Cleaning Cycle Required
shortDescription: The machine needs a cleaning cycle to remove coffee oil buildup from internal components.
fullDescription: The CLEAN indicator appears when the machine's internal counter determines a cleaning cycle is needed. Unlike descaling which removes mineral scale, the cleaning cycle uses tablets to dissolve and flush accumulated coffee oils.

causes.cause: Coffee oil accumulation in brew circuit

fixSteps.title: Insert cleaning tablet
fixSteps.instruction: Drop one Philips cleaning tablet into the pre-ground coffee chute (bypass doser), not into the bean hopper.

fixSteps.title: Start the cleaning cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Select the cleaning function from the maintenance menu on the display. Place a container under the spout.

fixSteps.title: Let the cycle run
fixSteps.instruction: The machine will dissolve the tablet and flush the brew circuit. This takes about 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Remove and clean brew group
fixSteps.instruction: After the cycle, remove and rinse the brew group under warm water for a thorough clean.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. Cleaning is always DIY.
videoSearchQuery: Philips espresso clean cycle tablet how to

faq.q: Where do I put the cleaning tablet?
faq.a: In the pre-ground coffee chute (bypass doser), never in the bean hopper. The tablet dissolves with water.

faq.q: How often is the cleaning cycle needed?
faq.a: Approximately every 500 cups, or when the CLEAN indicator appears.

faq.q: Is cleaning different from descaling?
faq.a: Yes. Cleaning removes coffee oils. Descaling removes mineral scale. Both are needed for optimal performance.

title: General Internal Error
shortDescription: The machine has encountered a general internal communication or hardware error.
fullDescription: Error 1 is a general fault code indicating a system-level issue. It can be caused by a temporary firmware glitch, a stuck mechanical component, or a sensor fault.

causes.cause: Brew group stuck in incorrect position

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the machine, unplug for 60 seconds, plug back in and turn on.

fixSteps.title: Remove and reinsert brew group
fixSteps.instruction: Open the service door, remove the brew group, and reinsert it to reset its position.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Navigate to Settings > Factory Reset on the display to reset all settings and clear error states.

fixSteps.title: Contact Philips support
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after reset, contact Philips customer support for further diagnosis.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and factory reset.

faq.q: Is Error 1 serious?
faq.a: Often not. A simple power cycle resolves most occurrences. It becomes serious if it recurs frequently.

faq.q: Will a factory reset lose my drink settings?
faq.a: Yes, all customized drink recipes and settings will be reset to defaults.

faq.q: How long is the Philips warranty?
faq.a: Philips offers a 2-year warranty on espresso machines in most regions.

title: Water Flow Error
shortDescription: The machine has detected abnormal water flow during brewing.
fullDescription: Error 5 indicates the flow meter detected abnormal water flow, either too fast or too slow. This typically means severe scale buildup, an air lock, or a pump issue.

causes.cause: Air lock in the water circuit

symptoms: Very slow or no water flow

fixSteps.title: Descale immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Error 5 often indicates severe scale. Run a full descaling cycle.

fixSteps.title: Prime the system
fixSteps.instruction: Run several water-only cycles to flush air from the system after descaling.

fixSteps.title: Clean the brew group
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and thoroughly clean the brew group. Old grounds can impede flow.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off, unplug for 60 seconds, and restart.

whenToCallPro: If the flow error persists after descaling, the flow meter or pump may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Philips espresso Error 5 flow water fix descale

faq.q: Can Error 5 be fixed at home?
faq.a: In most cases, yes. Descaling resolves the majority of Error 5 occurrences.

faq.q: What if Error 5 keeps coming back?
faq.a: If it returns quickly after descaling, you may have very hard water. Consider using the AquaClean filter and bottled water.

faq.q: Does Error 5 damage the machine?
faq.a: Continued use with severe scale can damage the pump and thermoblock. Resolve promptly.

title: AquaClean Water Filter Needs Replacement
shortDescription: The AquaClean water filter has reached its usage limit and needs replacement.
fullDescription: The AquaClean filter icon indicates the water filter has processed its maximum water volume and needs replacement for continued scale protection.

causes.cause: Filter has reached maximum usage (50 liters)
Filter not activated properly when installed

fixSteps.title: Prepare the new filter
fixSteps.instruction: Shake the new AquaClean filter, then submerge it upside down in water and press the filter 5 times to activate the carbon.

fixSteps.title: Install the new filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the water tank, remove the old filter from its holder, and insert the new activated filter.

fixSteps.title: Activate in the machine
fixSteps.instruction: On the display, go to AquaClean settings and select Activate/Reset. This resets the filter counter.

fixSteps.title: Run a flush cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Run one or two water-only cycles to flush the new filter.

videoSearchQuery: Philips AquaClean filter replacement activation how to

faq.q: How long does AquaClean filter last?
faq.a: Approximately 3 months or 50 liters of water.

faq.q: Do I still need to descale with AquaClean?
faq.a: With continuous AquaClean use, Philips claims you can go up to 5000 cups without descaling.

faq.q: Is AquaClean worth it?
faq.a: If your water is hard, AquaClean significantly extends the time between descaling and improves water taste.

title: Bean Hopper Empty or Beans Not Feeding
shortDescription: The machine detects no coffee beans or beans are not reaching the grinder.
fullDescription: The add beans indicator means the grinder is not receiving coffee beans. The hopper may be empty, the beans may be stuck (bridging), or the bean chute is blocked.
symptoms: Machine runs grinder but no coffee produced
Beans visible in hopper but not feeding

fixSteps.title: Fill the hopper
fixSteps.instruction: Add fresh coffee beans to the hopper.

fixSteps.title: Break the bean bridge
fixSteps.instruction: If beans are in the hopper but not feeding, use a spoon to stir and break any bridge formation above the grinder opening.

fixSteps.title: Clean the chute
fixSteps.instruction: Empty the hopper and use a vacuum to clean the bean chute of any oily residue or stuck bean fragments.

fixSteps.title: Use less oily beans
fixSteps.instruction: Very oily dark roasts tend to bridge more. Consider medium roast or less oily beans.

whenToCallPro: If the bean detection sensor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Philips espresso add beans not feeding bridging fix

faq.q: What is bean bridging?
faq.a: When beans form an arch above the grinder opening, preventing them from falling in. Stirring breaks the bridge.

faq.q: Are oily beans bad for my Philips?
faq.a: Oily beans can clog the grinder and chute. Use them occasionally and clean more frequently.

faq.q: Can I use pre-ground coffee instead?
faq.a: Yes, most Philips machines have a pre-ground coffee chute (bypass doser) for pre-ground coffee.

title: Drip Tray Full
shortDescription: The drip tray is full and needs to be emptied before the machine will continue.
fullDescription: The drip tray indicator activates when the float sensor detects the drip tray has reached its maximum water capacity. The machine will not brew until the tray is emptied.

causes.cause: Normal water accumulation from rinse cycles and brewing
Drip tray float stuck in up position
Float sensor magnet or reed switch fault

symptoms: Indicator appears immediately after emptying tray
Water visible at the float level

fixSteps.title: Empty the drip tray
fixSteps.instruction: Slide out the drip tray and the grounds container together. Empty the water and grounds. Wash with warm soapy water.

fixSteps.title: Check the float
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the red float in the drip tray. It should move freely up and down. Clean any residue preventing free movement.

fixSteps.title: Reinsert properly
fixSteps.instruction: Slide the drip tray and grounds container back in fully until they seat properly.

fixSteps.title: Always empty when machine is ON
fixSteps.instruction: The tray sensor reads properly only when the machine is powered on. Always empty while the machine is on.

whenToCallPro: Not needed unless float sensor has permanently failed.
videoSearchQuery: Philips espresso drip tray full indicator fix float

faq.q: How often should I empty the drip tray?
faq.a: Daily, or whenever the indicator activates.

faq.q: Why does the indicator come on immediately after emptying?
faq.a: The float may be stuck. Clean the float mechanism and ensure it moves freely.

faq.q: Is it normal for water to be in the drip tray?
faq.a: Yes, Philips machines drain rinse water into the drip tray during automatic rinse cycles.
